channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africans push for legalised medically assisted dying after years of suffering
item.description: For more than ten years, Dieter Harck, 76, has been battling for the right to decide how he dies, following his diagnosis with motor neuron disease, a debilitating and progressive condition. Harck exp...

item.title: President Ruto begins four-day outreach tour in Gusii region to strengthen local support
item.description: President William Ruto has kicked off a four-day development and political tour across the Gusii region, seen as a strategic move to expand his influence in an area that has often leaned towards the o...

item.title: Helen Zille highlights DA’s role in shaping South Africa’s political trajectory
item.description: Outgoing Democratic Alliance (DA) Federal Council chairperson Helen Zille delivered her final address at the party’s Federal Congress in Midrand, emphasising the party’s impact on South Africa’s polit...

item.title: Eastern US Faces Record-Breaking Heat Wave Next Week with temperatures reaching 34°C in Washington and 30°C in New York
item.description: A significant heat wave is expected to sweep across the eastern United States next week, bringing record-breaking temperatures to major cities including New York City and Washington, D.C. According to...

item.title: Low public participation slows progress in Kenya’s affordable housing savings scheme
item.description: The article from Nation.Africa reports that Kenya’s affordable housing fund has registered over 800,000 potential contributors, yet none have made any actual savings so far. This shows a worrying leve...

item.title: Retail Hedge Funds in South Africa Surpass Institutional Funds for the First Time
item.description: Over the past decade, South Africa's hedge fund industry has undergone a significant transformation. Once dominated by institutional investors and high-net-worth individuals, the sector has become inc...

item.title: Udhayanidhi Stalin urges Tiruchi electorate to support Secular Progressive Alliance
item.description: Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin on Saturday appealed to the electorate in Tiruchi to grant a decisive victory to the Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA) candidates in the ongoing Tamil Nadu As...

item.title: Fire at Navotas Landfill Impacts Malabon and Obando Residents
item.description: An inactive landfill in Navotas caught fire on the night of April 11, 2026, affecting nearby communities in Malabon and Obando, Bulacan, according to the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP). Firefighters ...

item.title: Gordon Injury Concerns After Reds Secure Thrilling Victory Over Crusaders
item.description: The Queensland Reds secured a thrilling 31-26 victory over the Crusaders in Super Rugby Pacific, but the win was bittersweet due to an injury to star player Carter Gordon. Gordon, a leading contender ...

item.title: Cardoso Balances Nostalgia with Sundowns' Champions League Focus in Tunisia
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns coach Miguel Cardoso is returning to Tunisia for the CAF Champions League semi-final first leg against Espérance de Tunis, a place where he holds fond memories and previously achieve...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch Criticises UK Crime Policies, Compares Youth Discipline to Nigeria
item.description: Kemi Badenoch, wey be leader of UK Conservative Party, don yarn say di way wey Labour Party dey handle crime no dey correct. For one article wey she write for Daily Mail UK, she talk say for Nigeria, ...

item.title: Advocates push for national autism care and inclusion law in the Philippines
item.description: Advocates and members of the autism community in the Philippines are pushing for the passage of a national law that would ensure genuine inclusion, care, and empowerment for people with autism spectru...

item.title: Majembe Comments on Mbavu Destroyer's Lawsuit over Kasarani Fight Result
item.description: Kenya's upcoming boxer Majembe has reacted to a lawsuit filed by Mbavu Destroyer against him and the event organiser, Oga Obinna, following their controversial bout at the Vurugu Fight held in Kasaran...

item.title: Farioli Defends Terem Moffi as Okoye Pursues Personal Milestone
item.description: Porto manager Francesco Farioli don stand behind Nigerian striker Terem Moffi after some of the home fans booed am during the 1-1 draw against Nottingham Forest in the Europa League match on Thursday....

item.title: DA Leadership Race: Horn Champions Provincial Representation
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is currently holding its Federal Congress in Midrand, Gauteng, where a hotly contested race for the position of Federal Council chair is underway. Werner Horn, a candidate...

item.title: Soo Jr. Greyhounds cleared to play championship game after dressing room dispute
item.description: The U18 AA Soo Jr. Greyhounds hockey team from Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, will compete in the regional championship game against the Copper Cliff Reds this Sunday in Sudbury after resolving a dispute ...

item.title: Rajnath Singh assures India is prepared as West Asia conflict continues despite ceasefire talks
item.description: Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh has emphasised that despite formal discussions around a ceasefire in the ongoing West Asia crisis, the situation on the ground shows that the conflict is far from ...

item.title: Tanzania bus services continue after government talks
item.description: Passengers across Tanzania can now breathe easy as the Tanzania Bus Owners Association (Taboa) has confirmed that bus services will continue without interruption. This decision comes after constructiv...

item.title: Melania Trump faces backlash after denying any link to Jeffrey Epstein in unusual press conference
item.description: Arwa Mahdawi's column in The Guardian explores Melania Trump's baffling decision to hold a press conference addressing rumours about her alleged connection with Jeffrey Epstein. The First Lady declare...

item.title: John Steenhuisen Reflects on DA Leadership and Transition to National Government
item.description: Outgoing Democratic Alliance (DA) leader John Steenhuisen delivered his final address at the party’s Federal Congress, reflecting on his leadership from 2019 to 2026. He recalled the party’s difficult...
